pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates patient dynamically via INSERT statement if not found in database; patient not present in pre-loaded patients.sql
Verified from setup_task.sh line 17: INSERT INTO demographics with DOB='1974-04-14'; Valid date (Sunday, April 14, 1974); Patient would be 52 years old on task date (2026-04-06)
Verified via Python datetime: April 6, 2026 is a Monday; matches task description requirement; verified in setup_task.sh line 30 and verifier.py line 39
Verified in setup_task.sh line 32 (start_time='09:00:00'); confirmed in verifier.py line 40 (expected_time_prefix = '09:00')
Verified via multiple sources: setup_task.sh lines 30-33 defines 4 dates; verifier.py line 39 expects 4 dates: ['2026-04-06', '2026-04-13', '2026-04-20', '2026-04-27']

Verified via Python datetime calculation: 2026-04-06→2026-04-13 (7 days), 2026-04-13→2026-04-20 (7 days), 2026-04-20→2026-04-27 (7 days); all Mondays; confirmed by 'weekly' scheduling mentioned in task description
Verified in task.json description: appointments for 'Smoking Cessation Counseling'; verified in verifier.py line 41: checks for these keywords in appointment reason field

privileged_info_summary: Task schedules 4 recurring weekly appointments for synthetic patient Eulalia Hammes (DOB 1974-04-14) for Smoking Cessation Counseling. Appointments are scheduled for Mondays: April 6, 13, 20, and 27, 2026, all at 09:00 AM. Patient is created dynamically at task setup (not pre-loaded); patient data is synthetic generated for this EMR task.
